mnemonics: ABS-TRACT: Abstract classes Think of Real-world objects, Acting as Contracts and Templates.
keyPoints: Abstraction hides complex implementation details and shows only essential features.
It is achieved using abstract classes and interfaces.
Abstract classes can have both abstract (unimplemented) and concrete (implemented) methods.
commonMistakes: Trying to instantiate an abstract class directly with the 'new' keyword.
Forgetting to implement all abstract methods from a parent abstract class in the concrete subclass.
Confusing abstraction with encapsulation; abstraction hides implementation, encapsulation hides data.
